source.task: Add Project Model v0 fixtures and a local advisory-signal harness for issue #3.

goal: Prove F1/F2/F3/F4 discrimination for pre-code Project Model reasoning without requiring live provider calls.
nonGoals: Do not redefine Project Model v0 locally.
Do not make Elenchus a truth oracle or production allow/deny gate.
Do not require live paid LLM/API calls for default verification.
Do not remove the existing patch-oriented fixtures unless replacement coverage is explicit.

components.name: Parent contract consumption
responsibilities: Consume schemaVersion project-model/v0 and the parent field semantics instead of inventing a local schema.

components.name: Project Model fixture manifests
responsibilities: Store proposal, public rationale, expected advisory signals, expected F-label, and label explanation per fixture.

components.name: Hermetic advisory-signal harness
responsibilities: Compare observed Elenchus advisory-signal fields to fixture expectations without live model calls.

components.name: README taxonomy and feedback notes
responsibilities: Move F3 language from patch-only framing to pre-code target, component, sequence, and example alignment.

dependencies.description: Fixture manifests target the parent contract before adding local checks.
The harness compares actual advisory signals against manifest expectations.
Harness results and fixture labels inform README taxonomy and issue feedback.

invariants.description: Default acceptance uses fixture-local or supplied JSON signals and local tests only.
Elenchus signal hints are advisory calibration evidence, not automatic truth or action gates.
F3 covers wrong target, component, sequence, level, or narrow example in pre-code reasoning, not only code patches.

observableChecks.description: Verify fixture docs name schemaVersion project-model/v0 and parent issue #2.
observableChecks.observableSignal: Fixture files and README refer to parent Project Model v0 rather than a local replacement.

observableChecks.description: Inspect fixture labels and confirm F1/F2/F3/F4 coverage with at least two F3 fixtures.
observableChecks.observableSignal: Manifest labels contain F1, F2, F3, F3, and F4.

observableChecks.description: Inspect expected advisory-signal JSON for component alignment, violations, gaps, alternatives, and F-label hints.
observableChecks.observableSignal: Each fixture has expected_advisory_signal.json with the Project Model advisory shape.

observableChecks.description: Run the local Project Model fixture checker.
observableChecks.observableSignal: The checker reports per-fixture signal matches and Project Model quality results without a single aggregate score.

observableChecks.description: Review README F3 wording for pre-code reasoning language.
observableChecks.observableSignal: README describes F3 beyond patch generalization and references Project Model fixture coverage.

evidenceRequirements.description: Terminal output from pytest and the Project Model fixture checker.
Manifest and signal excerpts showing labels and expected advisory fields.

assumptions.description: The parent Project Model v0 contract has been emitted on the build-arena issue branch.
Fixture-local observed signals are scripted stand-ins for default hermetic verification.

risks.description: The calibration repo could drift into owning a different local Project Model schema.
risks.mitigation: Keep parent issue and schemaVersion references in fixture data and docs.

risks.description: A fixture could blur F2 decorative rationale with F3 wrong-target rationale.
risks.mitigation: Store label explanations and expected advisory signal fields separately.

risks.description: A single overall score could hide which signal failed.
risks.mitigation: Report component, invariant, dependency, evidence, near-neighbor, and F-label mismatches separately.

nearNeighborAlternatives.description: Run live Elenchus or LLM providers by default.
nearNeighborAlternatives.whyNotPrimary: Default verification must remain hermetic and cost-free.

nearNeighborAlternatives.description: Keep F3 framed only as code patch generalization.
nearNeighborAlternatives.whyNotPrimary: The parent issue defines F3 as pre-code wrong target/component/sequence/example reasoning.

nearNeighborAlternatives.description: Only edit README taxonomy and call the project aligned.
nearNeighborAlternatives.whyNotPrimary: Issue #3 requires fixtures, expected signals, and a repeatable harness.

heldOutProbes.scenario: A fixture uses schemaVersion project-model/v0.1 or local field names.
heldOutProbes.expectedBehavior: The checker reports Project Model feedback rather than silently accepting the fixture.

heldOutProbes.scenario: A process or strategy proposal is real but violates ordering or component targeting.
heldOutProbes.expectedBehavior: Expected advisory signals classify it as F3, not F2.

heldOutProbes.scenario: An observed signal reports F1 for an expected F3 fixture.
heldOutProbes.expectedBehavior: The checker reports fLabelHint.label and component status mismatches.
